body: Patcher hosts other plugins as nodes on a canvas; you draw connections to set how audio and MIDI flow between them. Because there is no fixed slot count, an entire multi-plugin chain lives inside a single Patcher instance that you can save and reuse as one preset.

A patch can expose its own control surface - knobs, sliders and XY pads mapped to parameters inside the chain - so a complex network is played and automated as if it were one instrument or effect. Combined with frequency/amplitude splits, this makes Patcher a way to build reusable multiband racks.
